title: What to know as Texas votes in statewide, congressional runoffs
shortDescription: Texas voters head to the polls today for runoff races beyond the Senate fight, including attorney general, lieutenant governor and congressional contests.

title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns amid husband's cancer battle. See her career in Trump's cabinet
shortDescription: Tulsi Gabbard is preparing to resign from her position as Trump's Director of National Intelligence. See her career in politics.

title: Trump says he'll skip his son's wedding
shortDescription: Donald Trump's eldest son is reportedly set to wed socialite Bettina Anderson over the weekend, but the president said he won't be in attendance.

title: Trump intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard resigns, citing husband's cancer
shortDescription: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard is leaving her job due to her husband's rare bone cancer diagnosis.

title: Trump defends $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund amid GOP revolt
shortDescription: President Trump is digging in over the Justice Department's $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund amid criticism from some Republican senators.

title: Senate GOP ready to drop Trump's $1 billion ballroom security ask
shortDescription: Senate Republicans appear ready to strip $1 billion in White House security funding tied to Donald Trump's ballroom in a blow to the president.

title: Fetterman remains opposed to ending US war in Iran as measure advances
shortDescription: Sen. John Fetterman (D-PA) remained the sole Democrat opposed to ending the US war in Iran as the Senate voted on a war powers measure.

title: Trump sends 5,000 more US troops to Poland
shortDescription: President Donald Trump announces 5,000 more U.S. troops to Poland as NATO leaders meet and Poland reacts to the deployment.

title: Will President Trump attend Don Jr.'s wedding? Watch his response
shortDescription: President Donald Trump was asked May 21 if he is attending Donald Trump Jr.'s wedding. He said it was 'not good timing.' It's his son's second marriage.

shortDescription: President Donald Trump's eldest son is getting married over Memorial Day weekend. See Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson together in photos.

shortDescription: President Donald Trump said his son Donald Trump Jr.'s wedding to Bettina Anderson is "not good timing."

title: Could Jan 6th rioters benefit from Trump’s DOJ fund?

title: Is Jack Schlossberg leading this NYC congressional race? What to know
shortDescription: The nostalgia of Kennedy's short-lived "Camelot," now over six decades old, contrasts with modern American politics in 2026 around Trump and AI.

title: Republicans cancel vote on ending Iran war before measure could pass
shortDescription: Republican leaders in the House of Representatives abruptly canceled a scheduled war powers vote, fearing it would pass amid defections and absences.

title: Capitol police officers call DOJ fund brazen act of corruption
shortDescription: Capitol police officers are suing over a $1.776B DOJ fund tied to a Trump lawsuit, arguing it could benefit Jan. 6 defendants.

title: Spencer Pratt, social media and voter ID? New trend, explained
shortDescription: As claims circulate on social media around the Los Angeles mayoral race, here's how California voting works.

title: Trump's blamed an audit for not releasing his tax returns. Now it's over
shortDescription: The president has said since 2016 he can't release his taxes because of ongoing IRS reviews. With those gone, watchdogs say he's run out of excuses.

title: President Trump abruptly scraps plan to sign executive order on AI
shortDescription: President Donald Trump abruptly halted plans to issue an executive order on AI hours before tech executives were set to watch him sign it.

title: DNC 2024 election autopsy finally released
shortDescription: The Democratic National Committee released an autopsy explaining the 2024 presidential election loss to President Trump that has vexed liberals.

title: Trump admin announces Minnesota fraud cases
shortDescription: Trump Administration officials announced charges for fraud schemes that allegedly targeted $90 million in taxpayer dollars.

title: Fuming at Trump over 'slush fund,' Senate GOP skips town without passing ICE bill
shortDescription: Senate Republicans emerged stone-faced from a tense meeting with Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche. Then they decided to leave town.
